description: Delete LinkedIn project using OpenClaw role selector pattern

reasoning.research: LinkedIn uses dynamic React UI with CSS classes that change per session. Traditional selectors (aria-label, class names, IDs) all fail because they're not in the HTML or change dynamically.
reasoning.discovery: ARIA tree contains computed accessibility labels that are STABLE. Playwright role selectors (role=link[name='...']) use this computed ARIA, not HTML attributes.
reasoning.strategy: Use Playwright role-based selectors to find edit links by exact name. Pattern: role=link[name='Edit project {PROJECT_NAME}']. This bypasses all dynamic UI issues.
reasoning.openclaw_learning: Found this pattern in ~/projects/openclaw/src/browser/pw-tools-core.interactions.ts - they use getByRole('link', {name: '...'}) as the most stable selector strategy
reasoning.llm_learnings: Future LLMs should: (1) Always try role selectors first for dynamic UIs, (2) Check ARIA snapshot to find exact element names, (3) Use text-based selectors as fallback, (4) Never trust CSS classes on React sites

to_edit_modal.why_stable: Uses computed ARIA labels which don't change

to_delete_confirm.why_stable: Button text is stable for accessibility

execution_trace.purpose: Find exact project names and edit link labels

next_ai_instructions: To delete a LinkedIn project: (1) Navigate to /details/projects/, (2) Get ARIA snapshot to find exact project name, (3) Use role=link[name='Edit project {NAME}'] to click edit, (4) Use role=button[name='Delete'] twice (delete + confirm). Run in headless mode for Cloud Run deployment.
